rationale.R1.2: Checking the inferential chain from Table 2 numbers to the stated conclusions is central to the delegation. Critically, the paper treats a lower gain margin (2.1 dB vs 8.4 dB) as evidence of 'superior closed-loop stability,' which inverts the conventional control-theoretic interpretation — a higher GM denotes greater robustness to gain perturbations. Verifying that the logical steps connecting evidence to claim (ii) are valid is therefore a load-bearing sub-task, not merely peripheral.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation is precisely 'verify the evidence supports the claims.' This maps directly onto evidence-claim alignment: do the two Table 2 metrics (e_ss and GM) actually license claims (i) and (ii) as stated? This dimension is the primary and load-bearing responsibility; failure to assess it faithfully invalidates the entire delegation.
